Texas/Ohio held-out-target resolution: Austin fire CBA resolved and ingested; Austin now has two safety units (police, fire) but still lacks a matched non-safety comparison unit -- Austin non-safety remains dead weight for the matched-comparison design.

Houston Fire source-resolution run: ingested as an arbitration award (grievance/contract-interpretation, CBA Article 14 -- not Sec.174.1535 compulsory interest arbitration) plus incorporated Settlement Agreement and clarifying MOU, since the base CBA's full official text remains unlocated. Houston now has a healthy overlap-cycle match against tx_houston_other_2024 (HOPE, 2024-2027).

Texas second matched-city run: Austin EMS Association meet-and-confer agreement ingested as Austin's non-safety comparison partner. Austin police (2024-2029) and fire (2023-2025) both now have an overlapping-cycle non-safety match, making Austin Texas's second fully matched city alongside Houston. Note for report language: EMS is civil-service-protected and statutorily adjacent to police/fire (shared Ch.143 Civil Service Commission), not an ordinary civilian/clerical comparison unit.

No non-safety comparison unit identified for San Antonio in this expansion session (no confirmed negotiated non-safety CBA/bargaining channel found) -- unmatched for this project's core cross-occupation design; retained for institutional contrast value only (full Ch.174 bargaining without Houston's population-triggered compulsory-arbitration exception).
